briefdescription.para: The configuration table for TMR Inject devices.
The configuration table for TMR Inject devices.

detaileddescription.para: The receive and transmit FIFOs of the core are not flushed, so the user may want to flush them. The hardware device does not have any way to disable the receiver such that any valid data may be present in the receive FIFO. This function disables the core interrupt. The baudrate and format of the data are fixed in the hardware at hardware build time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.

is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific TMR Inject device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config. This function can initialize multiple instance objects with the use of multiple calls giving different Config information on each call.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use Config->BaseAddress for this parameters, passing the physical address instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything starts up as expected.

simplesect.para: The Config pointer argument is not used by this function, but is provided to keep the function signature consistent with other drivers.

briefdescription.para: This function enables fault injection for the indicated CPU.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.
is the CPU ID of the CPU where faults are to be injected. Must be 1 to 3.
is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to inject a fault in either:

listitem.para: An address bit of a memory or memory mapped register address for any read or write access on AXI or LMB.
A data bit written to memory or a memory mapped register on AXI or LMB, as well as output on an AXI Stream. <

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.
is the memory or register address, oro the data.
is the bit where the fault is injected. Must be 0 to 31.

simplesect.para: Either the unchanged value, or value with the fault injected for the CPU set up for fault injection.

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to inject any number of faults defined by the Mask parameter in either:

listitem.para: Any address bits of a memory or memory mapped register address for any read or write access on AXI or LMB.
Any data bits written to memory or a memory mapped register on AXI or LMB, as well as output on an AXI Stream. <

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.
is the memory or register address, oro the data.
defines the bits where faults are injected. A fault is injected for every bit in the mask set to 1.

simplesect.para: Either the unchanged value, or value with the faults injected for the CPU set up for fault injection.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.

is the unique id of the device controlled by this XTMR_Inject instance. Passing in a device id associates the generic XTMR_Inject instance to a specific device, as chosen by the caller or application developer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything starts up as expected.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device is not found in the configuration table.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the device hardware.

detaileddescription.para: Since there is no way to perform a loopback in the hardware, this test can only check the state of the status register to verify it is correct. This test assumes that the hardware device is still in its reset state, but has been initialized with the Initialize function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTMR_Inject instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the self-test was successful.
XST_FAILURE if the self-test failed, the status register value was not correct

briefdescription.para: Write a value to a TMRInject register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the TMRInject device.
is the register offset from the base to write to.
is the data written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTMR_Inject_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Write a value to a TMRInject register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the TMRInject device.
is the register offset from the base to write to.
is the data written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTMR_Inject_WriteReg64(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u64 Data)

briefdescription.para: Set the contents of the control register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XUL_CR_* constants defined above to create the bit-mask to be written to the register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the 32-bit value to write to the control register

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XTMR_Inject_SetControlReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Mask);

briefdescription.para: Contains required functions for the XTMR_Inject driver.

detaileddescription.para: The implementation of the XTMRInject component's static initialization functionality.
This file contains the self-test functions for the TMR Inject component (XTMR_Inject).
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
Contains data which is shared between the files of the XTMR_Inject component.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of TMR Inject devices in the system.
See the xtmr_inject.h header file for more details on this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release 1.1 mus 10/25/18 Updated XTMR_Inject_CfgInitialize to support 64 bit fault address. 1.4 adk 02/24/22 While updating the IIR offset apply the mask. 1.6 sa 01/05/23 Added support for Microblaze RISC-V. 1.6 asa 07/27/23 Updated to fix compilation error while initializing xtmr_inject_addr.

detaileddescription.para: Each device in the system should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xtmr_inject.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sa 04/05/17 First release
